brokers/internal/api_request_broker_cbor.nim
Line Branch Exec Source
1 ## API RequestBroker — CBOR mode codegen
2 ## --------------------------------------
3 ## Generates the CBOR-mode surface for `RequestBroker(API)` declarations.
4 ##
5 ## For each declaration this module emits:
6 ##
7 ## 1. The underlying multi-thread RequestBroker, exactly as the native path
8 ## does — providers register and run on the processing thread the same
9 ## way regardless of FFI mode. Internal cross-thread dispatch stays as
10 ## typed `Channel[T]` traffic; CBOR encoding only happens at the C ABI
11 ## boundary.
12 ##
13 ## 2. (When the signature has arguments) a synthetic per-request CBOR args
14 ## object that mirrors the parameter list field-by-field. Decoding the
15 ## foreign request buffer into this object gives us individual local
16 ## variables to forward into the broker's `request` call.
17 ##
18 ## 3. A CBOR adapter proc with the canonical signature
19 ##
20 ## proc <Type>CborAdapter*(ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]):
21 ## Future[seq[byte]] {.async: (raises: []).}
22 ##
23 ## The adapter decodes the request buffer (or ignores it for zero-arg
24 ## requests), `await`s the typed broker call, and encodes the resulting
25 ## `Result[T, string]` as a CBOR response envelope.
26 ##
27 ## 4. A compile-time entry in `gApiCborRequestEntries` so the upcoming
28 ## `registerBrokerLibrary` CBOR backend can wire the adapter into the
29 ## library's dispatch table.
30 ##
31 ## The wire `apiName` is the snake_case form of the response type name —
32 ## e.g. `InitializeRequest` becomes `initialize_request`. Foreign wrappers
33 ## are generated to use the same name so the C entry point sees a stable
34 ## identifier per broker.
35
36 {.push raises: [].}
37
38 import std/[macros, strutils]
39 import
40 ./helper/broker_utils,
41 ./mt_request_broker,
42 ./mt_config,
43 ./api_common,
44 ./api_cbor_codec,
45 ./api_schema,
46 ./api_type_resolver,
47 ./broker_debug
48
49 # `api_type_resolver` re-export: `autoRegisterApiType` is emitted into the
50 # user-library AST by the broker macros and must resolve at the user's
51 # expansion site. Previously this came in transitively via the native
52 # `api_request_broker` re-export chain (retired in Part A); re-export it
53 # explicitly here so user code never needs a direct
54 # `import brokers/internal/api_type_resolver`.
55 export mt_request_broker, mt_config, api_common, api_cbor_codec, api_type_resolver
56
57 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
58 # Schema registration
59 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60
61 proc registerCborObjectType*(
62 typeName: string, fieldNames, fieldTypes: seq[NimNode]
63 ) {.compileTime.} =
64 ## Register a parsed object type in `gApiTypeRegistry` so the C++ /
65 ## Python / etc. wrapper codegen can emit typed structs for it.
66 ## Idempotent — subsequent calls for the same type are a no-op so a
67 ## type that ends up registered through both the auto-resolver and a
68 ## broker macro doesn't double-list.
69 if isTypeRegistered(typeName):
70 return
71 var entry = ApiTypeEntry(name: typeName, kind: atkObject)
72 for i in 0 ..< fieldNames.len:
73 var fname = $fieldNames[i]
74 # `fieldNames` from parseSingleTypeDef carry the original AST,
75 # which for inline `object` types is a plain Ident (export marker
76 # already lifted by the parser). Strip a trailing '*' defensively.
77 if fname.endsWith("*"):
78 fname.setLen(fname.len - 1)
79 let ftype = fieldTypes[i].repr.strip()
80 entry.fields.add(ApiFieldDef(name: fname, nimType: ftype))
81 registerTypeEntry(entry)
82
83 proc registerCborPrimitiveType*(
84 typeName: string, parsed: ParsedBrokerType
85 ) {.compileTime.} =
86 ## Register a primitive (non-object) broker type — `type X = int32` — as a
87 ## distinct alias of its underlying primitive. Wrapper codegen then emits a
88 ## `using X = <prim>` alias and treats X as an emittable scalar payload (the
89 ## CBOR wire value is a bare scalar, not a map). A no-op for non-primitive
90 ## non-object types, which stay TODO-stubbed in the wrappers.
91 if isTypeRegistered(typeName):
92 return
93 if parsed.objectDef.kind == nnkDistinctTy and parsed.objectDef.len == 1 and
94 parsed.objectDef[0].kind == nnkIdent and isNimPrimitive($parsed.objectDef[0]) and
95 ($parsed.objectDef[0]).toLowerAscii() notin ["cstring"]:
96 # `string` is allowed (maps to the wrapper's native string type) so a POD /
97 # option-B `string`-payload request is emittable; `cstring` stays excluded
98 # (unsafe to marshal across the FFI/CBOR boundary).
99 registerTypeEntry(makeAliasEntry(typeName, $parsed.objectDef[0], atkDistinct))
100
101 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
102 # Adapter proc type — exposed so registerBrokerLibrary (CBOR mode) can
103 # materialise a uniform table of dispatchers.
104 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105
106 type CborApiAdapter* = proc(ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]): Future[seq[byte]] {.
107 async: (raises: []), gcsafe
108 .}
109
110 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111 # Helpers
112 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113
114 proc collectSignatures(
115 body: NimNode
116 ): tuple[
117 zeroArg: NimNode,
118 argSig: NimNode,
119 argParams: seq[NimNode],
120 zeroArgName: string,
121 argSigName: string,
122 ] {.compileTime.} =
123 ## Walk the macro body and split the (at most two) `signature*` proc
124 ## declarations into the zero-arg and arg-based slots, mirroring
125 ## `mt_request_broker` and the native path's handling.
126 result.zeroArg = nil
127 result.argSig = nil
128 result.argParams = @[]
129 result.zeroArgName = ""
130 result.argSigName = ""
131
132 for stmt in body:
133 if stmt.kind != nnkProcDef:
134 continue
135 let procName = stmt[0]
136 let procNameIdent =
137 case procName.kind
138 of nnkIdent:
139 procName
140 of nnkPostfix:
141 procName[1]
142 else:
143 procName
144 if not ($procNameIdent).startsWith("signature"):
145 error("Signature proc names must start with `signature`", procName)
146
147 let params = stmt.params
148 let paramCount = params.len - 1
149 if paramCount == 0:
150 result.zeroArg = stmt
151 result.zeroArgName = $procNameIdent
152 elif paramCount >= 1:
153 result.argSig = stmt
154 result.argSigName = $procNameIdent
155 for idx in 1 ..< params.len:
156 result.argParams.add(copyNimTree(params[idx]))
157
158 proc snakeApiName(typeIdent: NimNode): string {.compileTime.} =
159 ## Wire `apiName` for a request: snake_case form of the response type
160 ## identifier. e.g. `InitializeRequest` -> `initialize_request`.
161 toSnakeCase(sanitizeIdentName(typeIdent))
162
163 proc emitArgsType(
164 argsTypeIdent: NimNode, argParams: seq[NimNode]
165 ): NimNode {.compileTime.} =
166 ## Build `type <argsTypeIdent>* = object\n field1*: T1\n field2*: T2`
167 ## from the arg-based signature's parameter nodes.
168 ##
169 ## Each `argParams[i]` is an `nnkIdentDefs` node carrying one or more
170 ## names plus a type. We expand each name into its own field so an arg
171 ## like `(a, b: int32)` produces two separate object fields.
172 var recList = newNimNode(nnkRecList)
173 for paramDefs in argParams:
174 let lastIdx = paramDefs.len - 1
175 let typeNode = paramDefs[lastIdx - 1]
176 for nameIdx in 0 ..< lastIdx - 1:
177 let nameNode = paramDefs[nameIdx]
178 let fieldIdent =
179 case nameNode.kind
180 of nnkIdent, nnkSym:
181 ident($nameNode)
182 of nnkPostfix:
183 ident($nameNode[1])
184 of nnkPragmaExpr:
185 ident($nameNode[0])
186 else:
187 ident($nameNode)
188 recList.add(
189 newTree(
190 nnkIdentDefs, postfix(fieldIdent, "*"), copyNimTree(typeNode), newEmptyNode()
191 )
192 )
193
194 newTree(
195 nnkTypeSection,
196 newTree(
197 nnkTypeDef,
198 postfix(argsTypeIdent, "*"),
199 newEmptyNode(),
200 newTree(nnkObjectTy, newEmptyNode(), newEmptyNode(), recList),
201 ),
202 )
203
204 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
205 # Adapter emission
206 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
207
208 proc emitZeroArgAdapter(
209 typeIdent: NimNode, payloadType: NimNode, adapterIdent: NimNode, isVoid: bool
210 ): NimNode {.compileTime.} =
211 ## Adapter for a zero-argument request: ignore the input buffer, await
212 ## the broker call, encode the response envelope. `typeIdent` is the
213 ## dispatch tag; `payloadType` is the (decoupled) value type the request
214 ## resolves to and the envelope carries.
215 ##
216 ## For a `void` payload the public broker resolves to `Result[void, string]`,
217 ## which has no `Option[void]`-encodable envelope. We bridge it to the wire
218 ## unit type `CborUnit` (a zero-field map `{}`), matching the legacy
219 ## `type X = void` form bit-for-bit.
220 if isVoid:
221 quote:
222 proc `adapterIdent`*(
223 ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]
224 ): Future[seq[byte]] {.async: (raises: []), gcsafe.} =
225 discard reqBuf
226 let r = await `typeIdent`.request(ctx)
227 let unitR =
228 if r.isOk:
229 Result[CborUnit, string].ok(CborUnit())
230 else:
231 Result[CborUnit, string].err(r.error)
232 let envBytes = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(unitR)
233 if envBytes.isOk:
234 return envBytes.value
235 let errEnv = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(
236 Result[CborUnit, string].err("response encode failed: " & envBytes.error)
237 )
238 if errEnv.isOk:
239 return errEnv.value
240 return @[]
241
242 else:
243

typemappingtestlib::DualSigRequestCborAdapterArgs(broker_context::BrokerContext, seq<uInt8>):
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✗ Branch 1 not taken.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✗ Branch 3 not taken.
✗ Branch 4 not taken.
✗ Branch 5 not taken.
 6 let typeIdentName = $typeIdent
298 ✗ # A `void` payload resolves to `Result[void, string]` (no encodable
299 ✗ # envelope); bridge it to the wire unit type `CborUnit`, matching the
300 # legacy `type X = void` form. Every envelope on this path then carries
301 ✗ # `CborUnit`, and the awaited result is converted before encoding.
302 ✗ let envTypeName =
303 if isVoid:
304 ✗ "CborUnit"
305 ✗ else:
306 ✗ payloadType.repr.strip()
307 let argsTypeIdentName = $argsTypeIdent
308 ✗ let adapterIdentName = $adapterIdent
309 ✗
310 ✗ let encodeRespSrc =
311 if isVoid:
312 ✗ " let unitR =\n" & " if r.isOk: Result[CborUnit, string].ok(CborUnit())\n" &
313 ✗ " else: Result[CborUnit, string].err(r.error)\n" &
314 ✗ " let envBytes = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(unitR)\n"
315 else:
316 ✗ " let envBytes = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(r)\n"
317
318 let src =
319 "proc " & adapterIdentName & "*(\n" & " ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]\n" &
320 "): Future[seq[byte]] {.async: (raises: []), gcsafe.} =\n" &
321 " let decRes = cborDecode(reqBuf, " & argsTypeIdentName & ")\n" &
322 " if decRes.isErr:\n" & " let errEnv = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(\n" &
323 " Result[" & envTypeName &
324 ", string].err(\"request decode failed: \" & decRes.error)\n" & " )\n" &
325 " if errEnv.isOk:\n" & " return errEnv.value\n" & " return @[]\n" &
326 " let decoded = decRes.value\n" & " let r = await " & typeIdentName &
327 ".request(ctx" & argList & ")\n" & encodeRespSrc & " if envBytes.isOk:\n" &
328 " return envBytes.value\n" & " let errEnv = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(\n" &
329 " Result[" & envTypeName &
330 ", string].err(\"response encode failed: \" & envBytes.error)\n" & " )\n" &
331 " if errEnv.isOk:\n" & " return errEnv.value\n" & " return @[]\n"
332
333 parseStmt(src)
334
335 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
336 # reduced-A: create-instance adapters. When a request's Ok payload type is a
337 # registered BrokerInterface(API), the provider builds and returns a sub-
338 # interface ref. We do NOT CBOR-encode the ref; instead the adapter extracts
339 # the sub-instance's BrokerContext and encodes it as a bare `uint32` (the
340 # routing handle). The foreign wrapper decodes that ctx and constructs the
341 # typed sub-wrapper class. Adapter + provider both run on the processing
342 # thread (same-thread direct dispatch), so the ref never crosses a channel —
343 # safe under both --mm:refc and --mm:orc.
344 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345
346 proc emitZeroArgInstanceAdapter(
347 typeIdent, adapterIdent: NimNode
348 ): NimNode {.compileTime, raises: [ValueError].} =
349 let src =
350 "proc " & $adapterIdent & "*(\n" & " ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]\n" &
351 "): Future[seq[byte]] {.async: (raises: []), gcsafe.} =\n" & " discard reqBuf\n" &
352 " let r = await " & $typeIdent & ".request(ctx)\n" & " if r.isOk:\n" &
353 " installApiListenersForCtx(r.value.brokerCtx)\n" & " let mapped =\n" &
354 " if r.isOk: Result[uint32, string].ok(uint32(r.value.brokerCtx))\n" &
355 " else: Result[uint32, string].err(r.error)\n" &
356 " let envBytes = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(mapped)\n" & " if envBytes.isOk:\n" &
357 " return envBytes.value\n" & " return @[]\n"
358 parseStmt(src)
359
360 proc emitArgInstanceAdapter(
361 typeIdent, adapterIdent, argsTypeIdent: NimNode, argParams: seq[NimNode]
362 ): NimNode {.compileTime, raises: [ValueError].} =
363 var fieldNames: seq[string] = @[]
364 for paramDefs in argParams:
365 let lastIdx = paramDefs.len - 1
366 for nameIdx in 0 ..< lastIdx - 1:
367 let nameNode = paramDefs[nameIdx]
368 let nameStr =
369 case nameNode.kind
370 of nnkIdent, nnkSym:
371 $nameNode
372 of nnkPostfix:
373 $nameNode[1]
374 of nnkPragmaExpr:
375 $nameNode[0]
376 else:
377 $nameNode
378 fieldNames.add(nameStr)
379 var argList = ""
380 for f in fieldNames:
381 argList.add(", decoded." & f)
382 let src =
383 "proc " & $adapterIdent & "*(\n" & " ctx: BrokerContext, reqBuf: seq[byte]\n" &
384 "): Future[seq[byte]] {.async: (raises: []), gcsafe.} =\n" &
385 " let decRes = cborDecode(reqBuf, " & $argsTypeIdent & ")\n" &
386 " if decRes.isErr:\n" & " let errEnv = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(\n" &
387 " Result[uint32, string].err(\"request decode failed: \" & decRes.error))\n" &
388 " if errEnv.isOk:\n" & " return errEnv.value\n" & " return @[]\n" &
389 " let decoded = decRes.value\n" & " let r = await " & $typeIdent & ".request(ctx" &
390 argList & ")\n" & " if r.isOk:\n" &
391 " installApiListenersForCtx(r.value.brokerCtx)\n" & " let mapped =\n" &
392 " if r.isOk: Result[uint32, string].ok(uint32(r.value.brokerCtx))\n" &
393 " else: Result[uint32, string].err(r.error)\n" &
394 " let envBytes = cborEncodeResultEnvelope(mapped)\n" & " if envBytes.isOk:\n" &
395 " return envBytes.value\n" & " return @[]\n"
396 parseStmt(src)
397
398 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
399 # Public entry point
400 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
401
402 proc generateApiCborRequestBrokerImpl(
403 body: NimNode, cfg: MtReqCfg
404 ): NimNode {.raises: [ValueError].} =
405 ## Deferred-phase codegen for `RequestBroker(API)` under CBOR mode.
406 ## Runs after the typed-phase `autoRegisterApiType` calls have populated
407 ## `gApiTypeRegistry` for any external types referenced in the broker
408 ## response object or signature parameters (enums, distinct types,
409 ## nested objects). Wrapper codegen consumes that registry to emit
410 ## typed dataclasses / encoders / decoders.
411 result = newStmtList()
412
413 # 1. Emit the underlying MT broker (typed Nim<->Nim dispatch on the
414 # processing thread, identical to the native path). Capacity
415 # config flows in from the outer RequestBroker(API, ...) kwargs —
416 # same knobs as RequestBroker(mt).
417 result.add(generateMtRequestBroker(copyNimTree(body), cfg))
418
419 # 2. Determine dispatch tag, payload, signatures, and the schema parse,
420 # supporting both the legacy `signature*` form and the proc-sugar.
421 var hasSignatureProc = false
422 var hasOtherProc = false
423 for stmt in body:
424 if stmt.kind == nnkProcDef:
425 let nm = stmt[0]
426 let nmId = (if nm.kind == nnkPostfix: nm[1] else: nm)
427 if ($nmId).startsWith("signature"):
428 hasSignatureProc = true
429 else:
430 hasOtherProc = true
431 let isSugar = hasOtherProc and not hasSignatureProc
432
433 var typeIdent: NimNode = nil
434 var payloadType: NimNode = nil
435 var parsed: ParsedBrokerType
436 var zeroArgPresent = false
437 var argPresent = false
438 var argParams: seq[NimNode] = @[]
439 # Wire apiName suffixes. Legacy form keeps its descriptive
440 # `signature<Suffix>` mechanism (backward-compatible). The new proc-sugar
441 # uses the finalized rule: zero-arg stays bare, arg-based gets `_arg`.
442 var zeroApiSuffix = ""
443 var argApiSuffix = ""
444
445 proc legacySuffix(sigName: string): string =
446 if sigName.len <= "signature".len:
447 return ""
448 toSnakeCase(sigName["signature".len .. ^1])
449
450 if not isSugar:
451 parsed = parseSingleTypeDef(
452 body, "RequestBroker", allowRefToNonObject = true, collectFieldInfo = true
453 )
454 typeIdent = parsed.typeIdent
455 payloadType = copyNimTree(typeIdent)
456 let sigs = collectSignatures(body)
457 zeroArgPresent = not sigs.zeroArg.isNil
458 argPresent = not sigs.argSig.isNil
459 argParams = sigs.argParams
460 if zeroArgPresent and argPresent:
461 let zs = legacySuffix(sigs.zeroArgName)
462 zeroApiSuffix = (if zs.len > 0: "_" & zs else: "_zero")
463 let asfx = legacySuffix(sigs.argSigName)
464 argApiSuffix = (if asfx.len > 0: "_" & asfx else: "_args")
465 else:
466 let sg = parseRequestSugar(body, "RequestBroker", async = true)
467 typeIdent = sg.typeIdent
468 payloadType = sg.payloadType
469 parsed = sg.parsed
470 zeroArgPresent = not sg.zeroArgProc.isNil
471 argPresent = not sg.argProc.isNil
472 argParams = sg.argParams
473 if zeroArgPresent and argPresent:
474 argApiSuffix = "_arg" # zero-arg stays bare
475
476 let typeName = sanitizeIdentName(typeIdent)
477 let apiName = snakeApiName(typeIdent)
478
479 # reduced-A: does this request CREATE AND RETURN a sub-interface instance?
480 # (Its Ok payload type is a registered BrokerInterface(API).) If so the wire
481 # carries the sub-instance's ctx as a bare uint32 — we skip type registration
482 # (the interface ref is never CBOR-encoded) and emit instance adapters below.
483 let payloadName = payloadType.repr.strip()
484 let returnsIface = (if isApiInterface(payloadName): payloadName else: "")
485
486 # Register the payload type in the schema so wrapper codegen can emit
487 # typed structs / aliases. For the proc-sugar POD form this mirrors the
488 # legacy `type X = <prim>` registration exactly (wire-identical).
489 if returnsIface.len > 0:
490 discard # instance-returning request: no payload type to register.
491 elif parsed.hasInlineFields:
492 registerCborObjectType(typeName, parsed.fieldNames, parsed.fieldTypes)
493 elif parsed.isVoid:
494 # `void` → a zero-field object: payload-less request, the response
495 # envelope carries only the ok/err signal.
496 registerCborObjectType(typeName, @[], @[])
497 else:
498 registerCborPrimitiveType(typeName, parsed)
499
500 # Materialise (paramName, nimType) pairs from the arg-based signature so
501 # foreign-language wrapper codegen can emit a typed call signature.
502 proc paramFields(argParams: seq[NimNode]): seq[(string, string)] {.compileTime.} =
503 for paramDefs in argParams:
504 let lastIdx = paramDefs.len - 1
505 let typeNode = paramDefs[lastIdx - 1]
506 let typeStr = typeNode.repr.strip()
507 for nameIdx in 0 ..< lastIdx - 1:
508 let nameNode = paramDefs[nameIdx]
509 let nameStr =
510 case nameNode.kind
511 of nnkIdent, nnkSym:
512 $nameNode
513 of nnkPostfix:
514 $nameNode[1]
515 of nnkPragmaExpr:
516 $nameNode[0]
517 else:
518 $nameNode
519 result.add((nameStr, typeStr))
520
521 # 3. Emit adapters + register descriptors. Naming rule (replaces the old
522 # `_zero`/`_args`): single signature → bare apiName; both slots present →
523 # the zero-arg keeps the bare name, the arg-based gets the `_arg` suffix
524 # (`<broker>Arg` in the foreign wrappers).
525 if not zeroArgPresent and not argPresent:
526 # No explicit signature — treat as zero-arg, matching the native default.
527 let adapterIdent = ident(typeName & "CborAdapter")
528 if returnsIface.len > 0:
529 result.add(emitZeroArgInstanceAdapter(typeIdent, adapterIdent))
530 else:
531 result.add(
532 emitZeroArgAdapter(typeIdent, payloadType, adapterIdent, parsed.isVoid)
533 )
534 registerCborRequestEntry(
535 apiName, $adapterIdent, typeName, @[], returnsInterface = returnsIface
536 )
537 return
538
539 if zeroArgPresent:
540 let zeroAdapterTag = if argPresent: "Zero" else: ""
541 let adapterIdent = ident(typeName & "CborAdapter" & zeroAdapterTag)
542 if returnsIface.len > 0:
543 result.add(emitZeroArgInstanceAdapter(typeIdent, adapterIdent))
544 else:
545 result.add(
546 emitZeroArgAdapter(typeIdent, payloadType, adapterIdent, parsed.isVoid)
547 )
548 registerCborRequestEntry(
549 apiName & zeroApiSuffix,
550 $adapterIdent,
551 typeName,
552 @[],
553 returnsInterface = returnsIface,
554 )
555
556 if argPresent:
557 let argAdapterTag = if zeroArgPresent: "Args" else: ""
558 let adapterIdent = ident(typeName & "CborAdapter" & argAdapterTag)
559 let argsTypeIdent = ident(typeName & "CborArgs" & argAdapterTag)
560 result.add(emitArgsType(argsTypeIdent, argParams))
561 if returnsIface.len > 0:
562 result.add(
563 emitArgInstanceAdapter(typeIdent, adapterIdent, argsTypeIdent, argParams)
564 )
565 else:
566 result.add(
567 emitArgAdapter(
568 typeIdent, payloadType, adapterIdent, argsTypeIdent, argParams, parsed.isVoid
569 )
570 )
571 let fields = paramFields(argParams)
572 registerCborRequestEntry(
573 apiName & argApiSuffix,
574 $adapterIdent,
575 typeName,
576 fields,
577 returnsInterface = returnsIface,
578 )
579
580 when defined(brokerCoverage):
581 # Attribute the API (CBOR) RequestBroker layer — the underlying MT broker
582 # (idempotent re-stamp) plus the per-request CBOR adapter procs — onto the
583 # user's request type decl. Gated; normal builds unchanged.
584 for child in result:
585 stampLineInfo(child, typeIdent)
586
587 when defined(brokerDebug):
588 writeBrokerDebug(
589 "RequestBrokerApi", typeName, result, header = "apiName='" & apiName & "'"
590 )
591 when defined(brokerDebugStdout):
592 echo "[brokers/cbor] RequestBroker(API) for '" & typeName & "' (apiName='" &
593 apiName & "')"
594 echo result.repr
595
596 {.pop.}
597
598 macro generateApiCborRequestBrokerDeferred*(args: varargs[untyped]): untyped =
599 ## Typed-phase deferred codegen entry point. By the time this expands,
600 ## any preceding `autoRegisterApiType` calls have already populated
601 ## `gApiTypeRegistry`, so wrapper codegen can introspect external
602 ## enum / distinct / object types without falling back to TODO stubs.
603 ##
604 ## Args layout: [body, kw0, kw1, ...]. Kwargs are forwarded as raw
605 ## `nnkExprEqExpr` nodes from `generateApiCborRequestBroker` and
606 ## re-parsed here into an MtReqCfg.
607 if args.len == 0:
608 error("generateApiCborRequestBrokerDeferred requires a body", args)
609 let body = args[0]
610 var kwargs: seq[NimNode]
611 for i in 1 ..< args.len:
612 kwargs.add(args[i])
613 let cfg = parseMtReqKwargs(kwargs)
614 generateApiCborRequestBrokerImpl(body, cfg)
615
616 {.push raises: [].}
617
618 proc generateApiCborRequestBroker*(body: NimNode, kwargs: seq[NimNode]): NimNode =
619 ## Two-phase entry point — mirrors the native
620 ## `generateApiRequestBroker` pattern. Kwargs are passed through the
621 ## deferred macro call as raw nodes so the typed-phase expansion sees
622 ## the original literal values for `parseMtReqKwargs`.
623 result = newStmtList()
624
625 let externalIdents = discoverExternalTypes(body)
626 if externalIdents.len > 0:
627 result.add(emitAutoRegistrations(externalIdents))
628
629 let deferred =
630 newCall(ident("generateApiCborRequestBrokerDeferred"), copyNimTree(body))
631 for kw in kwargs:
632 deferred.add(copyNimTree(kw))
633 result.add(deferred)
634
635 {.pop.}
636
Created using GCOVR (Version 8.5) at 2026-06-06 00:35:57
